title: pyrus cydonia maliformis
intro: Pyrus cydonia maliformis, commonly known as the Chinese quince or Japanese quince, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia, characterized by its showy, white flowers and sweet, golden-yellow fruit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, which have been found to aid in wound healing and reducing fever. The health benefits of Pyrus cydonia maliformis include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional cooking and as a natural dye.
